column2: Fill In
ZTBrowser core trust platform
Browser-visible attestation verification, provenance lookup, canonical enclave release discipline, and repeatable AWS Nitro deployment automation.
Platform/security engineers, auditors, and demo operators using a browser extension plus operator tooling.
Attestation is usually backend-oriented, hard to interpret in the browser, and difficult to tie to auditable release provenance and repeatable deployment.

ZTBrowser verifies attestation in the browser, resolves verified PCRs to public provenance metadata, anchors canonical identity to a dedicated enclave release pipeline, and supports operator-driven AWS deployment automation.
attestation_verified, facts_lookup_matched rate, canonical release freshness, deploy success rate, deploy-to-verification time

As a user, I want the extension to show a lock only when attestation is valid and otherwise show a visible failure reason, so that I do not trust unverifiable services.
Visit site -> extension fetches /.well-known/attestation -> verify in extension runtime -> popup shows locked/unlocked with debug context

US-2
As a reviewer, I want verified runtime identity to resolve to auditable provenance and canonical release evidence, so that I understand what was measured and how it was published.
Extension verifies PCRs -> facts lookup resolves metadata -> reviewer inspects repo/release -> canonical release artifacts and rebuild-verification remain available

The extension stores factsMatched=true and workload metadata

Canonical provenance shall be reproducible and auditable

Operator / platform engineer
US-3
As an operator, I want a staged deploy-and-verify workflow with visible logs and controlled cleanup, so that attested services can be demonstrated and operated repeatably.
Choose release tag and AWS profile -> run verify/deploy via scripts or ztdeploy -> watch stage logs -> inspect/manage live deployments
SC007
The AWS profile and release tag are configured and required scripts are present
The operator runs the verify flow
The workload is deployed, verified, and then stopped or terminated based on cleanup settings
release tag, AWS profile, cleanup mode
Stage logs, HTTP verification result, attestation verification result
verified deployment run

Operators shall be able to deploy and verify a canonical release via scriptable AWS automation
The operator UI shall show stage-level progress and live logs
Requirement
Deployment automation shall minimize accidental cloud cost
Operator logs shall remain available after the run
